implementation: qk-native GLSL source; no external SPIR-V or binary loaded; dn_step_batch_cols and fa_attn_batch_coopmat* reimplement decompositions understood from llama.cpp shader source (gated_delta_net.comp, flash_attn_cm1.comp) with attribution comments

finding: QK_PB_BUSY_PATH polling forces a >=100 ms idle between repetitions (the engine's own busy tail triggers the retry sleep), so every rep starts with down-clocked auto-DPM. llama-bench runs repetitions back-to-back hot. Same binary, same flags: 192.5 ms (cold protocol) vs 173.8 ms (hot) at pp512 = 11%. Prior-campaign qk numbers therefore carried a cold-clock penalty llama's reference numbers never paid. Final numbers are reported under BOTH protocols; hot protocol verifies gpu_busy_percent==0 immediately before each 5-rep block (second sysfs read authoritative: the first read after runtime-PM idle returns a stale value) and VRAM residency excludes co-tenants.

note: pp512 side-by-side that reframed the campaign: qk stage times (QK_COUNTERS, ~245 ms span) vs llama.cpp 571d0d5 GGML_VK_PERF_LOGGER warm pass (172.3 ms total). qk already WON routed gate/up (59.0 vs 63.4 ms) and glue (~4 vs ~21.4 ms); the losses were dn.step 32.4 vs GATED_DELTA_NET 6.3, at.core 17.1 vs FLASH_ATTN_EXT 1.6, dense projections 55.9 vs ~36, shared/router 19.7 vs ~7.8. The previous residual attribution treated dn.step as immovable after five tiling variants; the llama comparison showed the parallel decomposition, not the tiling, was wrong.

name: dn_step_batch_cols: column-sharded batched delta rule
detail: one wave64 WG per (head, 8-column group), LANES=8 lanes shard each 128-float state row (16 floats/lane) with clustered subgroup reductions; 512 WGs vs 32, pure-f32 reassociation only
result: dn.step 32.4->5.0 ms; LANES sweep 4/8/16 = 226.1/224.2/225.5 ms pp512; ids4 EXACT from layer 0 at both admission geometries

name: fa_attn_batch_coopmat: 16x16x16 KHR coopmat flash attention (Br=16, Bc=64)
result: at.core 17.1->6.6 ms; prefillcmp worst rel 0.039; ids4 EXACT from layer 0

name: fa_attn_batch_coopmat2: Br=32, Bc=32 twin (halved K/V read amplification)
result: ABAB vs Br16: pp512 172.5 vs 173.9/173.6 ms; pp2048 680.7/682.2 vs 719.1/721.3 ms (-38 ms); ids4 bisection: first layer 0 and 8 DIVERGE the rollback trajectory at generated index 32 (onto llama's ref4 token 13914, i.e. it would 'fix' the pre-existing ref4 divergence — still a rollback-gate violation per the brief), layer 4 EXACT at both geometries
decision: retain as default, first layer 4; Br16 kernel kept as QK_ATTN_PREFILL_COOPMAT_BR16 rollback

name: gemm_q8_0_coopmat_split: hi/lo split-precision dense GEMM below the f16 coopmat boundary (layers <10)
detail: both operands split into f16 hi/lo planes, lo scaled 2^11, three cooperative products — the recipe proven on the shared down projection
result: ABAB on/off at first-layer 4: 201.2/201.5/201.7/200.6 vs 211.5/211.6/211.1/211.4 ms (-10.2 ms); ids4 bisection: 0/2/6 DIVERGE, 4 EXACT both geometries

name: moe_logits_gemm: tiled batched router logits (64x32 f32 tiles)
detail: replaces one-256-thread-WG-per-(expert,token) GEMV grid (131072 WGs at N=512, router matrix re-read per token)
result: -11.5 ms pp512 at first-layer 0; ids4 bisection: 0/4/6 DIVERGE, 8 EXACT both geometries

name: gemm_q8_0_coopmat2_bm64: 64-row dense tile for short-M shapes (bit-identical schedule)
result: ABAB WASH at pp512 (192.5/192.5 vs 192.4/192.4 ms) — the dn.out grid-tail occupancy hypothesis was wrong (seventh wrong bottleneck hypothesis in this project)
decision: reject as default; kernel kept opt-in behind QK_PREFILL_COOPMAT_BM64

backend: qk Vulkan (tuned + dn cols + coopmat FA Br32@L4 + split dense@L4 + logits gemm@L8)
protocol: hot (busy==0 verified before each block, reps back-to-back — same conditions as llama-bench)

backend: qk Vulkan rollback (tuned flags only, contemporaneous, hot)

prefillcmp_full_tuned_flags: 0/42 pre-existing harness limitation, unchanged (identical without candidate flags)
ids4: candidate==rollback EXACT at maxB 1024 and 512; pre-existing ref4 divergence at idx 32 (735 vs 13914) unchanged in the retained config
n128_handoff: HANDOFF EXACT (seeds 1234/7/99; seed 1234 produces 0 tokens both ways, pre-existing)
block_ablock: pre-existing CPU-reference threshold failures byte-identical before/after (block 33.1 / ablock 18.0, state max-abs 2.38e-07)

result: prefill parity reached and exceeded: 1.001x/1.086x/1.034x vs contemporaneous llama.cpp at pp512/pp1024/pp2048 (hot protocol both sides); rollback->candidate same-session gains +33.9%/+37.5%/+47.7%
attribution: the gap was never the routed MoE (qk already beat llama there): it was (1) DeltaNet batch step using 32 WGs with 128 floats of state per lane — column-sharding to 512 WGs at 16 floats/lane cut it 32.4->5.0 ms, (2) scalar attention core vs coopmat flash attention (17.1->~5 ms, and K/V amplification at long base), (3) scalar dense projections below the f16-coopmat precision boundary (split-precision hi/lo recovered layers 4-9), (4) per-token router-logits GEMVs (batched f32 GEMM from layer 8)
remaining: pp512 is parity-not-victory: attention core is still ~3x llama per op (f32 KV cache forces staging conversions llama avoids with an f16 KV cache), dense layers 0-3 remain scalar-order-locked, and moe.gate+up ~60 ms is now the largest single stage on both engines
